steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor
steps.content: Square A's sides are 166 times square B's sides. The linear scale factor is 166.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule
steps.content: When linear dimensions scale by a factor $k$, areas of similar figures scale by $k^2$. With $k = 166$, the area of square A is $166^2$ times the area of square B.
